summary: Decompiled Luigi fireball item code by replacing asm/melee/it/items/itluigifireball.s with src/melee/it/items/itluigifireball.c and switching obj_files.mk to build the C object. The PR report says the 868-byte file reached 99.89% overall, with every listed function at 100% except it_802C0368 at 99.68%. Supporting changes cleaned up item headers, added the Luigi fireball include to Luigi SpecialN, widened itUnkAttributes for special attributes used by the item, and made sqrtf_accurate available as a static inline in lbvector.h for the velocity-length comparison.
what_changed: Deleted the 291-line assembly file asm/melee/it/items/itluigifireball.s and added a 218-line C implementation in src/melee/it/items/itluigifireball.c.
Updated obj_files.mk so TEXT_O_FILES uses $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/it/items/itluigifireball.c.o instead of the old asm object.

Implemented or exposed Luigi fireball routines including it_802C01AC, it_802C027C, it_802C02E4, it_802C031C, it_802C0368, it_802C0490, it_802C0498, it_802C04B8, it_802C04C0, it_802C04C8, it_802C04D0, and it_802C04F0.
Defined ItemStateTable it_803F75C0 in C as a one-entry item state table using it_802C02E4, it_802C031C, and it_802C0368.
Moved the it_802C01AC prototype out of the broad it_27CF.h and into the dedicated it/items/itluigifireball.h, then included that header from ftLg_SpecialN.c.

Expanded itUnkAttributes in itCommonItems.h from two floats to four floats, adding x8 and xC; the fireball code uses x0_float for horizontal speed, x4_float for lifetime, and xC as a velocity-length threshold.

Reworked it_266F.h by adding baselib/forward.h, using address-commented prototypes, adding needed item prototypes such as it_8026D9A0, it_80272860, it_80273030, itColl_BounceOffShield, and it_8026B894, and moving a large documentation/prototype dump into new it_266F.dox.
Changed lbvector.h from the old _lbvector_h_ guard to GALE01_00D2EC, switched it to forward headers, and moved sqrtf_accurate from an extern inline in lbvector.c to a static inline in the header.
Added include cleanup for itfoxillusion.c, including switching from angle-bracket melee includes to local quoted includes and adding it/it_2725.h.

Updated small support declarations/types: it_2725.h now declares it_802750F8 and it_8027781C, Item.xBC0 changed from unk_t to UNK_T, lbcollision.c includes GXTransform.h, and tools/convert_data.py temporarily targets **/itluigifireball.s instead of **/ground.s.
smart_moves: Kept the difficult near-match function it_802C0368 in a hybrid form: readable C is present for normal/non-MUST_MATCH or WIP builds, while an inline-assembly fallback preserves exact codegen under the guarded path.

Used sqrtf_accurate and VEC2_SQ_LEN for the velocity-length comparison in it_802C0368, matching the original frsqrte/Newton iteration pattern closely enough that only this function carried the remaining 24-point mismatch.
Rehomed the public fireball spawn prototype to itluigifireball.h and updated Luigi SpecialN to include that item-specific header, reducing reliance on a broad catch-all item header.
Added address comments to prototypes in it_266F.h and itluigifireball.h, improving searchability and matching auditability without renaming unknown symbols prematurely.
Moved sqrtf_accurate into lbvector.h as static inline so other translation units can reuse the same codegen-sensitive implementation rather than duplicating or relying on an extern inline definition.
Preserved suspected stack/codegen artifacts with explicit MUST_MATCH-only unused stack buffers and comments such as '@todo Unused stack' rather than hiding unexplained matching hacks.
decomp_lessons: For item article decompilation, specialAttributes structs may need to be expanded from observed use sites before giving fields semantic names; this PR kept x0_float and x4_float and added x8/xC conservatively.

When a single function prevents a full match, it can be useful to land the file as a 99% C decomp with an ASM guarded fallback for the hard function instead of blocking all surrounding 100% functions.

Item spawn helpers often require carefully initializing SpawnItem fields, including parent gobj fields, prev_pos.z, velocity components, facing_dir, damage, and x44 flags, to reproduce stack layout and behavior.
Effect-spawn calls may expose uncertain varargs or hidden parameters; this PR explicitly left a '@todo Missing #efAsync_Spawn param?' comment around efAsync_Spawn usage in it_802C0368.
Avoid leaving item-specific APIs in broad umbrella headers when a dedicated item header exists; move the declaration and update the fighter code to include the narrower header.
Large prototype/header reorganizations should retain address annotations because they make future matching work and symbol lookup easier even when names remain unknown.
If an inline math helper is codegen-sensitive, keeping it in a header as static inline can make it available to multiple decompiled files while preserving inlining behavior.
naming_conventions: Unknown item functions retained address-style names such as it_802C01AC and it_802C0368.
Dedicated item headers use the item file name, e.g. src/melee/it/items/itluigifireball.h, rather than exposing item-specific functions through it_27CF.h.
Address comments were added in headers using forms such as /* 2C01AC */ for functions and /* 3F75C0 */ for data.
Boolean item callbacks use bool return types where behavior is known, e.g. it_802C02E4, it_802C0368, it_802C0490, it_802C0498, and it_802C04D0.
The header guard for lbvector.h was normalized to a GALE01 address-style guard, GALE01_00D2EC.
Uncertain struct fields remained conservative, e.g. itUnkAttributes.x0_float, x4_float, x8, and xC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The original asm showed it_802C0368 using frsqrte plus four Newton refinement iterations; the C version reused sqrtf_accurate to model that sequence.
The matching report identifies it_802C0368 as the only non-100% function: 296 bytes, score 24, 99.68%.
The C implementation includes MUST_MATCH-only unused stack arrays in it_802C01AC and it_802C0368 to account for otherwise unexplained stack layout.
it_802C0368 is declared with ASM and includes an inline assembly body under the guarded fallback path, preserving the original PowerPC sequence when exact matching is needed.
The C object replaced the asm object in obj_files.mk, making the decompiled file participate in the normal build.

tools/convert_data.py was pointed at **/itluigifireball.s, suggesting local data-conversion tooling was used for this item slice, but the PR context does not show why this temporary glob change was committed.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish or rename it_802C0368 in itluigifireball.c.
Search for subsequent changes to itUnkAttributes.x8 or itUnkAttributes.xC to identify Luigi fireball article attribute meanings.
Check whether tools/convert_data.py was reverted from **/itluigifireball.s back to a general/default input glob in a later PR.
Look for later cleanup of the '@todo Missing #efAsync_Spawn param?' comment and efAsync_Spawn signature.
Find other item PRs that reuse sqrtf_accurate or the it_802C0368 hybrid C/ASM pattern.
